evidence_quotes.quote: video games have been destroyed or deactivated

paragraphs.text: The protection of consumer rights when it comes to having products from the video games sector has been torpelled and nourished by the industry, also led by the lobby who sees consumers only as a credit card with a name. To date, hundreds or thousands of video games have been destroyed or deactivated due to poor management or bad habits brought to this sector by avaria. What we are looking for with the ‘Stop Killing/Destroying Games’ initiative is to ensure that companies do not go with clean hands when they leave or, directly, abuse end-user’s licence agreements, taking into account that many of the decatalogued, deactivated or destroyed titles are too expensive to maintain, when it has been proven very often that this is a fallacious, and that a permanent solution is found to a problem that can be uncontrolled in the long term.
